metric_value: Service organizations just beginning a major cost restructuring a la US manufacturing 30 years ago.
Buyer dissatisfaction with IT service providers still high.
Companies seeking lower-cost resources themselves (insourcing).
Integrators seeing no movement in large, long-timeframe work.
CXO discomfort with value propositions of IT service providers.
Larger providers and ERP vendors re-discovering all-inclusive hosted solutions as means to deliver value, flexibility, transformation and scalable solutions.
Appropriate support for global clients now that more clients than ever are global.
Offshore labor arbitrage is a key PSO issue.
Bench optimization is a key PSO issue.
Marketing of services is a key PSO issue.
Virtuous cycle: Bench strength + intellectual property + marketing.
BPO/On Demand are new account control mechanisms that adversely affect many service providers.
Motivating consulting staff now that careers aren't what they used to be.
Stack wars: customers buy fast, complete, scalable, transforming solutions — not software or hardware alone.
Account control slipping from technology component providers to large integrators (Microsoft -> IBM).
Global restructuring of service operations.
Rise of Linux as services market driver.
Transform business processes with Internet services.
Make vs. Buy transition going back to 'make' — buyers seeking more value and commoditization.
Threats: commoditization of services, software, and hardware.
Linux named as a threat to services providers.
HP named as a competitive threat.
IBM named as a competitive threat.
Europe and Asia ABM named as threat.
Stage 1: Embrace every possible partner in-country; use partners to sell/install/support; on-site service personnel negligible.
Stage 2: Formalize partner program; cull marginal partners; hire in-country pre-sales and post-sales services.
Stage 3: Build out service organization significantly; take more control of client implementations; dramatically cull partner count.
Stage 4: Drop most partners except those developing add-on modules/products; focus on extraordinary complementary capabilities.
Open question to Dell: 'Where is Dell in this continuum?'

Services ecosystem mapped on 'magnitude of effort on customer' vs 'nature of project' (tactical/strategic): Tech Support, Upgrades, Traditional Implementations, BPO/BTO, Hosted Apps, Strategic Consulting.
Tactical Partners: sell/install basics; focus on lowering TCO; highly interchangeable; often possess similar skills as Dell Services personnel.
Strategic Partners: design & sell new solutions; extend technologies for new apps; create new markets; focus on client ROI not TCO; complement (not repeat) Dell skill sets; deep industry knowledge.
Partner behaviors tied to solution focus (package vs custom) along risk and tactical/strategic axes.
